mechanism_narrative: Persistently low UNRATE signals tight labor markets that sustain consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year mean 20-day SPX return during periods when UNRATE is below 5.0% is not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1500 signals the onset of a housing recession, which historically precedes broad economic contraction and negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked at 2273 in Jan 2006 and have been declining
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1500, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or average return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized month-over-month declines in both INDPRO and RSAFS for two consecutive months indicate a contraction in production and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS fall MoM for two consecutive months
failure_condition: In triggered periods, 60-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of cases or the average return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 3%), a low FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY < 3%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day SPX returns is positive or the mean return when FEDFUNDS is in lowest quartile is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE greater than 0.2 percentage points signals imminent labor market weakening, which depresses consumer confidence and leads to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, 5-day SPX returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances or mean return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in RSAFS indicates robust consumer spending, which directly feeds into corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.1 or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Combined housing weakness (HOUST < 1600) and high interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) exacerbate credit stress and recession fears, producing strongly negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day SPX returns are negative in fewer than 65% of cases or average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During sustained economic expansion (GDPC1 year-over-year growth above 2%), rising INDPRO confirms robust manufacturing activity and supports positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In expansion regime, the correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or mean return when INDPRO rises is not significantly positive.
